_comment: Near-miss calibration: same-couple BETTER vs WORSE pick pairs, authored fresh (not the obvious off-genre strawmen — both picks in each pair are on-genre and plausible). 'clear' = good vs mediocre; 'subtle' = good vs slightly-better. The judge scores both picks under the SAME context; we measure ordered-pair accuracy (does better outscore worse?) and the score-gap distribution to establish the judge's RESOLUTION FLOOR — the minimum gap that signals a real difference vs the judge's discrimination noise. A Step-3 lift only counts if its per-couple gap clears that floor.

pairs.p1mood: Bleak, high-stakes apocalyptic survival with real weight
pairs.p2mood: Grounded sci-fi with stakes, open to spectacle

better.title: Children of Men
better.note: bleak apocalyptic dread — the exact axis P1 leaned into

worse.title: Snowpiercer
worse.note: very good apocalyptic sci-fi but more action-forward, a touch off the bleak-survival mood

better.title: Hereditary
better.note: slow-burn psychological dread — exactly what P1 swiped toward

worse.title: The Conjuring
worse.note: strong horror but more conventional jump-scare craft than the psychological dread mood

p1Yes: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

better.title: Arrival
better.note: high-concept AND emotionally weighty — threads P1's cerebral-emotional lean with P2's puzzle

worse.title: Inception
worse.note: excellent mind-bender but more action-spectacle, a touch less of P1's quiet emotional depth

better.title: Sicario
better.note: bleak and morally grey — nails P1's mood, tense enough for P2

worse.title: The Town
worse.note: solid crime thriller but a more conventional heist, less of the bleak moral greyness

better.title: Se7en
better.note: dread-soaked and sinister — both swiped it; bullseye for both moods

worse.title: Zodiac
worse.note: excellent but more procedural-drama than visceral dread; slightly less of P2's horror edge

pairs.p2mood: Tense crime thriller with a real twist

better.title: Prisoners
better.note: morally heavy crime thriller both shortlisted

worse.title: Now You See Me
worse.note: on-genre but a shallow popcorn twist-thriller — clearly a notch below for this couple

better.title: Crazy, Stupid, Love.
better.note: warm, witty, genuinely smart rom-com both shortlisted

worse.title: How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days
worse.note: on-genre but a more formulaic, lightweight rom-com — clearly a notch below

better.title: Inside Out
better.note: heartfelt and witty — both shortlisted; nails both moods

worse.title: The Boss Baby
worse.note: animated comedy but lightweight and far less heartfelt — clearly a notch below
